page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the N1 server family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations position this instance in the lower performance tiers. The single-core stress-ng score of 1103.99 and Geekbench single-core score of 936.0 are categorized as weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Multi-core performance is poor, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1451.07 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1106.0, both placing in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, measuring 13.5 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 11.46 GB/sec for large block write operations.

Despite its modest performance profile, the n1-standard-2 offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation rather than shared-core burstable resources. It is suitable for lightweight applications such as static web serving, which achieves a throughput of 1.6 GB/sec, or basic caching tasks, with Redis SET operations reaching 1.39 million operations per second. Other appropriate use cases include development environments, small database operations, and low-demand background utilities.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-2 is an entry-level general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz, this instance allocates 2 threads per physical core with dedicated CPU resources. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate that the n1-standard-2 resides in the lower tiers, with a Geekbench single-core score of 936.0 and a multi-core score of 1106.0, both placing in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, reaching 13.5 GB/sec for large-block reads. Given its qualitative cost efficiency as an older-generation dedicated resource, this instance is best suited for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, development environments, small-scale Redis caching, and basic database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It is designed for lightweight workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor.
tagline: A budget-friendly, general-purpose virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon resources for lightweight workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor
Dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
Cost-effective option for basic database and caching tasks
